arguments.query: Google Ads API authentication OAuth2 developer documentation
Google Ads API access requirements self-serve developer credentials
Google Ads MCP server agent toolkit
Google Ads API developer token basic access standard access application process self-serve
Google Ads API free tier pricing developer token cost

researched_category.notes: Google Ads is Google's online advertising platform for creating, managing, and optimizing digital campaigns.

one_liner.value: Google Ads is an online advertising platform that lets businesses create, manage, and optimize digital ad campaigns across Google's network.

methods.notes: OAuth 2.0 is the primary authentication protocol. Additionally, each request requires a developer token (22-char alphanumeric string) sent as an HTTP header, which functions as an API key identifier.

access.notes: Initial sign-up for a developer token is self-serve through the API Center of a Google Ads manager account. You may receive Test Account Access or Explorer Access automatically. To upgrade to Basic Access (15k ops/day) or Standard Access (unlimited), you must submit an application with a 5-10 business day review process. The API itself is free to use, but you need a Google Ads account (which may require ad spend). Explorer Access (introduced 2026) allows limited production access without formal application.

summary.value: The Google Ads API (v24) is a comprehensive REST/gRPC API with hundreds of services covering campaign management, ad groups, keywords, audience targeting, billing, reporting (via Google Ads Query Language), recommendations, conversion tracking, asset management, and more. It supports streaming responses, batch operations, and has official client libraries for Python, Java, .NET, Ruby, PHP, Perl, Node.js, and Go.

agent_interface.notes: Google publishes an official MCP server (google-ads-mcp) on GitHub under the googleads organization, with documentation on the Google for Developers site. It currently provides read-only tools: search, get_resource_metadata, list_accessible_customers. Composio offers a managed Google Ads toolkit with 9 tools including mutate operations (create/update/remove campaigns and ad groups). The official SDKs and client libraries also support programmatic agent integration.

rationale.value: The Google Ads API is fully documented, broad, and has an official MCP server and SDKs. However, building an agent toolkit today requires obtaining a developer token with appropriate access level. While Test Account Access and Explorer Access are self-serve, production use requires Basic or Standard Access which involves an application review process (5-10 business days for Basic, 10 business days for Standard). The Explorer Access level (auto-granted in some cases) allows limited production use (2,880 ops/day) with restricted features, which is sufficient for basic agent toolkits. The main blocker is that a developer token tied to a Google Ads manager account is required, and higher access levels require approval. For a simple agent toolkit the Explorer Access level may be sufficient, but for full functionality the Basic/Standard Access application process creates a gate.

s1.quote: Google Ads is Google's online advertising platform for creating, managing, and optimizing digital campaigns. It helps businesses reach targeted customers and maximize return on ad spend.

s2.quote: Google Ads Application Programming Interface (API) is an advanced feature that lets advertisers interact with and make changes to their Google Ads account through applications they create.

s3.quote: Google Ads API utilizes the OAuth 2.0 protocol for authentication and authorization, allowing your app to access user accounts without handling login information.

s4.quote: In addition to OAuth 2.0 credentials, a developer token is also required to make Google Ads API calls.

s5.quote: The developer token is a 22-character long alphanumeric string that lets your app connect to the Google Ads API.

s6.quote: After you've completed the initial sign-up for the Google Ads API, you're issued a developer token with the Test Account Access level. ... After you've completed the initial sign-up for the Google Ads API, Google may automatically upgrade your developer token from Test Account Access level to the Explorer Access level in some cases.

s7.quote: Basic Access level allows the developer token to execute up to 15,000 operations per day. ... How to apply: Instructions. How long do reviews typically take? 5 business days

s8.quote: Google acknowledged significant delays in processing developer token access applications for its Google Ads API on February 6, 2026, revealing a backlog that has affected review timelines for Basic Access and Standard Access level requests.

s9.quote: Google Ads API v24 - Reference ... A service for managing account-level budgets through proposals. ... Service to manage ads in an ad group. ... Service to manage campaigns. ... Service to fetch data and metrics across resources.

s10.quote: A developer token is required to make calls to the Google Ads API and is a 22-character alphanumeric string obtained from the API Center page of your Google Ads manager account.

s11.quote: The Google Ads MCP server provides a standardized bridge to the Google Ads API, which lets AI agents analyze and retrieve campaign data using natural language.

s12.quote: This repo contains the source code for running an MCP server that interacts with the Google Ads API.

s13.quote: Securely connect your AI agents and chatbots (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, etc) with Google Ads MCP or direct API to monitor campaign performance, update ad groups, generate reports, and launch new ads through natural language.
